decision_rule: ship the verified-loop opt-in iff the Fable-Loop arm's hidden-test pass rate exceeds the prompt-matched-solo control by >= 8 percentage points AND its total token cost is within <= 25% of that control; else park. A null (no gain) result is published verbatim, not tuned away.
primary_metric: hidden-test pass rate on the G0.2 opus-fixture executable oracles (fraction of the 12 hidden oracles that PASS), scored by the frozen fixture, arm-blind
co_primary_metrics: total tokens per arm (G0.1 cost instrumentation)
wall-clock ms per arm (G0.1 cost instrumentation)
judge_id: frozen-executable-oracle (no LLM judge — pass rate is decided by G0.2 hidden oracles, not preference)
margin: 8pp pass-rate gain vs prompt-matched-solo at <= 25% token overhead
guard_metrics: atomic-task unnecessary-work penalty: on the atomic single-file tasks, the Fable-Loop arm is DOCKED if it adds loop/verifier/resume work the one-shot arm did not need (loop must not tax easy tasks)
control_rationale: The prompt-matched-solo arm gets the SAME acceptance criteria as the Fable-Loop arm but no durable state / no verified-completion loop. If Fable-Loop only matches solo, the gain was the criteria, not the loop. Comparing against plain-opus alone would overstate the loop's value (charter #1 / harness-paradox caution).
hard_preconditions: G0.2 one-shot Opus baseline attestation MUST be recorded first (fixture must have headroom — if plain-opus already saturates the hidden oracles, the A/B cannot discriminate and the fixture is re-hardened + re-frozen before this experiment runs)
elon budget go-ahead for real Opus token spend (owner decision — HUMAN_GATE Stage 3)
note: Pre-registered BEFORE any run (registered_at precedes first_run_at). The RUN is elon-budget-gated; this file fixes the decision rule so the result cannot be reframed after the numbers are in.
